rag_rules: Fix type mismatches between AGREE expressions and AADL port/data types by aligning declarations with visible identifiers.
Ensure AGREE contracts are placed in valid component type or implementation scopes based on the visibility of referenced features.
Avoid introducing package-qualified or undefined identifiers in AGREE annexes; use only those present in the AADL model.
generic_rules: Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations in AGREE.
Write guarantees that relate visible outputs to visible inputs, avoiding implementation-only subcomponent paths.
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they refer to component ports.
Validate that all AGREE expressions use compatible types (int, real, bool) as defined by the AADL model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Use `assume`, `guarantee`, `eq`, `const`, and `assign` with correct syntax and scoping rules.
Branching logic in AGREE should use nested `if ... then ... else` expressions.
anti_patterns: Do not reference subcomponent ports or implementation-level features in a component type annex.
Do not introduce undefined or package-qualified identifiers in AGREE contracts without matching AADL declarations.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
AGREE validator diagnostics and repair strategies
Model Analyst agent's role in extracting AADL structure
